prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens on a very dark, low-contrast image. The visual details are difficult to make out; only vague shadowy shapes are visible, and no clear objects, people, or text can be identified from the sampled frame.

By the final frame, the commercial has transitioned to a clear product display. Several cylindrical lip-care tubes are arranged around a central package labeled “softlips” and “lip protectant.” The tubes also show the “softlips” name vertically. Above the products, on-screen text reads: “Lip protection for soft healthy lips.” At the bottom, smaller text reads: “Use only as directed.” The background is a soft gray gradient, and the product is lit in a polished studio-style presentation.
